User request:
Show a bar chart of count sales by month where customer_age > 100

Dataframe columns:
month: category
segment: category
sales: float
profit: float
customer_age: int

import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.header("Count Sales by Month")

df_filtered = df[df["customer_age"] > 100]

df_plot = df_filtered.groupby("month", as_index=False)["sales"].count()

fig = px.bar(
    df_plot,
    x="month",
    y="sales",
    template="plotly_white"
)

fig.update_layout(height=500)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Plot a scatter plot of profit vs units colored by region where region == East with a Streamlit selectbox for region

Available columns:
sales: float
region: category
profit: float
revenue: float
units: int

import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.subheader("Profit vs Units")

df_filtered = df[df["region"] == "East"]

options = ["All"] + sorted(df["region"].dropna().unique().tolist())
selected_region = st.selectbox("Region", options)
if selected_region == "All":
    df_filtered_widget = df_filtered
else:
    df_filtered_widget = df_filtered[df_filtered["region"] == selected_region]

plot_fig = px.scatter(
    df_filtered_widget,
    x="profit",
    y="units",
    color="region",
    template="plotly_white"
)

plot_fig.update_layout(height=500)

st.plotly_chart(plot_fig, use_container_width=False)
Generate a Streamlit app snippet using plotly.express only. Assume df already exists. Return code only.

User request:
Create a heatmap of count by product and region with a Streamlit selectbox for segment

Columns in df:
region: category
segment: category
month: category
customer_age: int
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.title("Count of Product vs Region")

options = ["All"] + sorted(df["segment"].dropna().unique().tolist())
selected_segment = st.selectbox("Segment", options)
if selected_segment == "All":
    df_widget = df
else:
    df_widget = df[df["segment"] == selected_segment]

df_plot = df_widget.groupby(["product", "region"], as_index=False).size().rename(columns={"size": "count"})

fig = px.density_heatmap(
    df_plot,
    x="product",
    y="region",
    template="simple_white"
)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)
Generate a Streamlit app snippet using plotly.express only. Assume df already exists. Return code only.

User request:
Build a heatmap of count by segment and product where region == East with a Streamlit selectbox for region

Dataframe columns:
product: category
customer_age: int
year: int
segment: category
region: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.subheader("Count of Segment vs Product")

df_filtered = df[df["region"] == "East"]

options = ["All"] + sorted(df["region"].dropna().unique().tolist())
selected_region = st.selectbox("Region", options)
if selected_region == "All":
    df_filtered_widget = df_filtered
else:
    df_filtered_widget = df_filtered[df_filtered["region"] == selected_region]

df_plot = df_filtered_widget.groupby(["segment", "product"], as_index=False).size().rename(columns={"size": "count"})

chart = px.density_heatmap(
    df_plot,
    x="segment",
    y="product",
    template="plotly_white"
)

st.plotly_chart(chart, use_container_width=True)
Generate a Streamlit app snippet using plotly.express only. Assume df already exists. Return code only.

st.title("Max Units by Month")

)

Columns in df:
year: int
segment: category
date: datetime
units: int
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.subheader("Sum Units over Date")

options = ["All"] + sorted(df["product"].dropna().unique().tolist())
selected_product = st.selectbox("Product", options)
if selected_product == "All":
    df_widget = df
else:
    df_widget = df[df["product"] == selected_product]

df_plot = df_widget.groupby("date", as_index=False)["units"].sum()

fig = px.line(
    df_plot,
    x="date",
    y="units",
    color="product",
    template="simple_white"
)

fig.update_layout(height=500)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)
Generate a Streamlit app snippet using plotly.express only. Assume df already exists. Return code only.

st.subheader("Max Revenue by Year")

df_filtered = df[df["region"] == "West"]

options = ["All"] + sorted(df["segment"].dropna().unique().tolist())
selected_segment = st.selectbox("Segment", options)
if selected_segment == "All":
    df_filtered_widget = df_filtered
else:
    df_filtered_widget = df_filtered[df_filtered["segment"] == selected_segment]

df_plot = df_filtered_widget.groupby("year", as_index=False)["revenue"].max()

fig = px.bar(
    df_plot,
    x="year",
    y="revenue",
    template="plotly"
)

fig.update_layout(height=500)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)
Generate a Streamlit app snippet using plotly.express only. Assume df already exists. Return code only.
